title: biscutella boetica
intro: Biscutella boetica, also known as the Pyrenean kidney vetch or Pyrenean biscutella, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. It is native to the Pyrenees and surrounding regions. Biscutella boetica has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties, which are believed to help with various health issues such as urinary tract problems, kidney stones, and certain types of arthritis. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or dried extracts, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
